title: loadng.c: RREQ hop_count field not used for path length tracking
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/loadng.c

Protocol issue: RREQ uses hop_limit (decremented as message travels) but doesn't track accumulated hop_count. When the destination generates RREP, it starts with hop_count=0 (line 578).

In lichen_loadng_process_rreq() line 561-570:
    struct lichen_loadng_route reverse = {
        .hop_count = 0,  // <-- Always 0, doesn't track how many hops RREQ traveled
        ...
    };

When installing the reverse route, hop_count is always 0 regardless of how many hops the RREQ actually traversed. This means:
1. All reverse routes appear to be 0 hops away
2. Route comparison/selection cannot prefer shorter paths
3. The metric field is also 0

Per LOADng spec, RREQ should accumulate hop count as it forwards, and RREP should inherit this information for accurate path length tracking.
close_reason: RREQ now accumulates hop_count from 0; reverse route uses rreq->hop_limit+1; forward increments; fixes path length for gradients/cache; commit b1091d694
Updated RREQ reverse route to use hop_count=1 for correct path length tracking
